action.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 scissors, 2 lemons, 2 bananas

Your secret value function is:

scissors: 0, lemon: 2, banana: 3

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content:
I value the bananas most (3), then lemons (2), and scissors least (0). Since there are two bananas and I only need one to maximize my utility, I should aim to get one banana. The other player likely values bananas similarly or at least wants to secure one. To ensure a fair and successful split where both get something valuable, I will propose taking one banana. This leaves the other player with the remaining items (1 scissors, 2 lemons), which should be valuable enough for them to accept, assuming they value lemons similarly.

I propose: [1 banana]

(Note: Since I cannot see the other player's values, I am making a reasonable assumption that they value lemons and bananas positively. If they value bananas more than I do, they might take a banana, but taking one banana myself guarantees I get my highest value item. If the other player takes a banana, I will need to adjust, but for now, I will proceed with this offer to initiate the agreement.)

Wait, I need to be careful. If I say "[1 banana]" in the chat, the system might interpret it as a secret proposal if it looks like the format. The instructions say: "Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted... Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal."

So, I must communicate my intent without square brackets. I will propose that I take one banana and ask if they agree to take the rest.

Message to other player:
I want 1 banana. Do you agree to take the remaining items (1 scissors, 2 lemons)?
action.label: response
